session.user:
➜ erk-slot-58 git:(plnd/docs-v2-code-reviews-03-12-1314) uv sync error: Workspace member `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-58/packages/erkbot` is missing a `pyproject.toml` (matches: `packages/*`)
is there a way we can make this project resilient to this error
session.assistant: Let me explore the workspace configuration and the packages directory structure.

total 0 drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 3 09:19 . drwxr-xr-x@ 38 schrockn staff 1216 Mar 12 13:06 .. drwxr-xr-x@ 7 schrockn staff 224 Mar 9 15:28 erk-dev drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 9 16:14 erk-mcp drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 9 15:28 erk-shared drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 9 15:28 erk-statusline

total 0 drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 12 13:16 . drwxr-xr-x@ 38 schrockn staff 1216 Mar 12 13:15 .. drwxr-xr-x@ 7 schrockn staff 224 Mar 12 13:15 erk-dev drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 12 13:15 erk-mcp drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 12 13:15 erk-shared drwxr-xr-x@ 6 schrockn staff 192 Mar 12 13:15 erk-statusline
session.assistant:
The issue is clear: `members = ["packages/*"]` is a glob that matches any directory under `packages/`, so if a stray directory (like `erkbot`) appears without a `pyproject.toml`, `uv sync` breaks.
Let me check if there's any history or context around how stray directories end up in worktrees.
session.tool_result: 789a61ff3 Add lightweight HTTP webhook server (Starlette) b52c4ab8f Add erkbot system prompt module with erk-aware agent instructions (#8125) f1d66180f Add suggested reply buttons to erk-slack-bot chat responses e101420b8 Add plan: Plan: Node 1.7 — Create prompts.py with erk-specific system prompt 24cd5e27e Add ErkBot class with streaming agent support and emoji lifecycle management (#8087)

tool_use.param: Check if erkbot was ever tracked

session.tool_result: dd35477c1 Delete erkbot package (#8658) 682d6747a Delete erkbot package 01531d947 Loosen Pydantic version constraints to >=2.0 (#8629) 5208ccf82 WIP: Prepare for PR submission d2b0c8599 Add plan: Plan: Simplify Learn Trigger in `erk land` 47fc86659 Fix erkbot test RuntimeWarnings from unawaited coroutines (#8203) 6a8528193 Fix erkbot test RuntimeWarnings from unawaited coroutines (#8203) 135b3841a Fix erkbot test RuntimeWarnings from unawaited coroutines (#8203) 789a61ff3 Add lightweight HTTP webhook server (Starlette) 339412228 Address PR review comments: extract helper variables for clarity

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: ls -la /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-58/packages/erkbot/ 2>/dev/null || echo "erkbot dir not found (already cleaned up)"
Check if erkbot still exists in the worktree

session.tool_result: erkbot dir not found (already cleaned up)
session.assistant:
Now I have the full picture. `erkbot` was a package that was deleted (commit `dd35477c1`), but its directory persisted in the worktree after rebasing. This is a common git behavior — deleted directories can linger as empty dirs after branch operations.
The fix is simple: switch from the glob pattern to explicit members. Let me write the plan.

tool_use.name: Write
tool_use.param:
# Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories
## Context
The `pyproject.toml`...[truncated 1117 chars]... (no pyproject.toml) and verify `uv sync` still succeeds 3. Clean up `packages/fake-pkg/`

session.assistant: One-line change: replace the `packages/*` glob with explicit member paths. This makes `uv sync` ignore stray directories that lack a `pyproject.toml`.

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param: [{'tool': 'Bash', 'prompt': 'run uv sync to verify workspace configuration'}]

# Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories
## Context
The `pyproject.toml`...[truncated 1117 chars]... (no pyproject.toml) and verify `uv sync` still succeeds 3. Clean up `packages/fake-pkg/`

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/drifting-herding-finch.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

1→# Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The `pyproject.toml` uses `members = ["packages/*"]` glob for workspace members. When a worktree has a stray directory under `packages/` without a `pyproject.toml` (e.g., `packages/erkbot` lingering after branch operations), `uv sync` fails with: 6→ 7→``` 8→error: Workspace member `.../packages/erkbot` is missing a `pyproject.toml` 9→``` 10→ 11→This happens because git doesn't always clean up empty directories when switching/rebasing branches. In erk's worktree-heavy workflow, this is a recurring hazard. 12→ 13→## Change 14→ 15→In `pyproject.toml`, replace the glob with explicit members: 16→ 17→```toml 18→# Before 19→[tool.uv.workspace] 20→members = ["packages/*"] 21→ 22→# After 23→[tool.uv.workspace] 24→members = [ 25→ "packages/erk-dev", 26→ "packages/erk-mcp", 27→ "packages/erk-shared", 28→ "packages/erk-statusline", 29→] 30→```
... [15 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
**Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories**
**Problem:** `members = ["packages/*"]` glob matches any directory under `packages/`, so stray directories without `pyproject.toml` (like `packages/erkbot` lingering after a rebase) break `uv sync`.
**Fix:** Replace the glob with explicit members in `pyproject.toml`:
```toml [tool.uv.workspace] members = [ "packages/erk-dev", "packages/erk-mcp", "packages/erk-shared", "packages/erk-statusline", ] ```
**Tradeoff:** Must update `pyproject.toml` when adding/removing packages (rare — you'd already be editing it for the dependency).
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories\nCurrent context: ...[truncated 389 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `pr_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <pr_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<pr_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<pr_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → PR #<pr_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `pr_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <pr_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<pr_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Let me save the plan as a draft PR.
**Branch slug:** `explicit-uv-workspace-members`
**Summary:** Replaces the packages/* glob in uv workspace members with explicit package paths, preventing uv sync failures when stray directories without pyproject.toml appear in worktrees after branch operations.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f" --bra...[truncated 143 chars]...hen stray directories without pyproject.toml appear in worktrees after branch operations."

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "pr_number": 9219, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9219", "title": "[erk-pr] Plan: Make uv workspace resilient to stray directories", "branch_name": "plnd/explicit-uv-workspace-memb-03-12-1318", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/df6af5c8-1b52-421a-bf0c-81c41567034f/plan_snapshots/000001-89df4c6b"}
session.assistant: Plan saved. Now pushing the planning session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec push-session --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn-cod...[truncated 60 chars]...1a-bf0c-81c41567034f" --stage planning --source local --pr-number 9219 2>/dev/null || true
